```{r}
n_pairs_G2020<-length(levels(na.omit(pair_IDs_G2020)))
my_palette_G2020<-brewer.pal(n = max(3,n_pairs_G2020), name = "Paired") 

# Map each vertex to the correct color
pair_colors_G2020<- my_palette_G2020[pair_IDs_G2020]
pair_colors_G2020[is.na(pair_colors_G2020)] <- "gray80"
```

##big plot
```{r}
plot(gfilter_G2020, edge.width = E(gfilter_G2020)$weight*6,edge.curved=F,vertex.color =pair_colors_G2020, vertex.size = 15, vertex.label.cex = 0.7)
pair_levels_G2020<- levels(na.omit(pair_IDs_G2020))
legend_colors_G2020 <- my_palette_G2020[seq_along(pair_levels_G2020)]

legend(
  "topleft",
  legend = c(paste("Pair", pair_levels_G2020), "random pair"),
  col    = c(legend_colors_G2020, "gray80"),
  pch    = 19,
  pt.cex = 2,
  bty    = "n"
)
```
###edge list
```{r}
edge_G2020<-  igraph::as_data_frame(g_G2020, what="edges")

vtype_G2020<- V(g_G2020)$type
names(vtype_G2020) <- V(g_G2020)$name

from_type_G2020<-vtype_G2020[ edge_G2020$from ]
to_type_G2020<-vtype_G2020[ edge_G2020$to   ]

edge_G2020$pair_type <- ifelse(
  from_type_G2020== "mother-daughter" & to_type_G2020 == "mother-daughter",
  "mother-daughter",
  "other"
)

```

### SRI plot
```{r}
sri_G2020<- as.vector(net_G2020)
sri_G2020 <- sri_G2020[sri_G2020 > 0]  # Remove zero values
meansri_G2020<- mean(sri_G2020)


hist(sri_G2020, breaks=30, main="Distribution of association index (SRI) for GB2 in 2020", 
     xlab="Association index (SRI)", ylab="Frequency", col="lightblue", border="black",xlim = (0:1))

#red line for the mean
abline(v=meansri_G2020, col="blue", lwd=2)
```
Two different hist for the two types of pairs
```{r}

```
## EDGE PERMUTATION
Metrics of interest for quantifying: 
1- The mean edge weight among mother–daughter vertices
2- the difference in average association between mother–daughter pairs versus all other pairs

1-
observed value
```{r}
mean_pair_association <- function(g) {
  pairid <- V(g)$pairID #new variable with the pair ID
  names(pairid) <- V(g)$name #get the pair ID individual's name 
  #find the pairID of both individual
  from_pairid<- pairid[ edge_G2020$from ]
  to_pairid<- pairid[ edge_G2020$to   ]
  
  #has to have the same ID and not NA
  MDpairs <- (from_pairid == to_pairid) & !is.na(from_pairid)
  
  #isolate the weight of association and measure mean
  md_weights <- edge_G2020$weight[MDpairs]
  mean(md_weights, na.rm=TRUE)
}
obs_value_G2020<- mean_pair_association(g_G2020)
```

permutation
```{r}
num_permutations<- 5000
perm_E_G2020<-numeric(num_permutations)

for (i in seq_len(num_permutations)) {
  # Randomly permute the pair_type labels
  permuted_edge_list<-edge_G2020
  permuted_edge_list$pair_type <- sample(edge_G2020$pair_type)
  
  # Calculate the statistic for permuted mother-daughter pairs
  permuted_md_edges <- permuted_edge_list[permuted_edge_list$pair_type == "mother-daughter", ]
  perm_E_G2020[i] <- mean(permuted_md_edges$weight, na.rm = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
pvalue_e_G2020<-mean(perm_E_G2020>= obs_value_G2020)

cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2020,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(perm_E_G2020),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_e_G2020, "\t")
if (pvalue_e_G2020< 0.05) cat("significative") else
  cat("not significative")

```
```{r}
hist(perm_E_G2020, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(edge label permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0.2,0.4))
abline(v=obs_value_G2020, col="red", lwd=3)
```
Good, however: maybe a vertex-label permutation would be better than a edge-label because mother-daughter is a fundamental property of individuals.

In edge-label, we shuffle which edges are labeled "MD", and in a vertex, we would shuffle the type of the vertex and then redefine the MD edges

vertex-label permutations best capture the idea that we’re testing whether these specific mother–daughter individuals have unusually strong connections compared to any random subset of individuals
But we would measure all the mother with all the mothers association?

## VERTEX PERMUTATION

I already calculated the observed value

permutation
```{r}
original_pairid_G2020<- V(g_G2020)$pairID

num_perm<- 5000
perm_V_G2020<- numeric(num_perm)

for (i in seq_len(num_perm)) {
  #shuffle the entire vector of pairIDs
  new_pid<- sample(original_pairid_G2020, replace = FALSE)
  
  #copy the original graph and assign the new id
  g_perm <- g_G2020
  V(g_perm)$pairID <- new_pid
  
  #mean association
  perm_V_G2020[i] <- mean_pair_association(g_perm)
}

```

```{r}
pvalue_v_G2020<- mean(perm_V_G2020>= obs_value_G2020)
cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2020,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(perm_V_G2020),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_v_G2020, "\t")
if (pvalue_v_G2020< 0.05) cat("significative") else
  cat("not significative")
```

```{r}
hist(perm_V_G2020, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(vertex label permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0,0.6))
abline(v=obs_value_G2020, col="red", lwd=3)
```

##DATA RANDOMIZATION 
(randomization of the entire network, then calculation of the differences between md pairs and non-md pairs to see if it is different than what is observed)
mean of association for mother-daughter pairs is already measured

permute
```{r}
network_perm_G2020<-network_permutation(association_data=GBI_G2020,data_format = "GBI",permutations=5000,association_index = "SRI",association_matrix = net_G2020,identities=colnames(GBI_G2020),days= 1:nrow(GBI_G2020))

```

```{r}
individuals_G2020<-colnames(GBI_G2020)
dimnames(network_perm_G2020)[[2]] <- individuals_G2020
dimnames(network_perm_G2020)[[3]] <- individuals_G2020
#network_perm[1,,]

md_mean_from_adj <- function(adj_mat, md_pairs) {
  # 'adj_mat': a 26x26 adjacency matrix with row/col names = individual IDs
  # 'md_pairs': a data frame with columns "mother", "daughter" 
  #    e.g. mother = "B587", daughter = "5672", etc.

  n_pairs <- nrow(md_pairs)
  vals <- numeric(n_pairs)

  for (i in seq_len(n_pairs)) {
    mother_id <- md_pairs$mother[i]
    daughter_id <- md_pairs$daughter[i]

    # match them to the adjacency
    row_idx <- match(mother_id, rownames(adj_mat))
    col_idx <- match(daughter_id, colnames(adj_mat))

    # If they exist in the names, get the weight
    # If either is NA, the pair is not found, so set to NA
    if (!is.na(row_idx) && !is.na(col_idx)) {
      vals[i] <- adj_mat[row_idx, col_idx]
    } else {
      vals[i] <- NA
    }
  }

  # Return the mean edge weight across all real MD pairs
  mean(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
}

```

```{r}
#measure the association only for the md in the entire permutations
MD_perm_G2020<-apply(network_perm_G2020, 1, function(adj_mat) {
  md_mean_from_adj(adj_mat, mother_daughter_pairs_G2020)
})

pvalue_N_G2020<- mean(MD_perm_G2020>= obs_value_G2020)

cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2020,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(MD_perm_G2020),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_N_G2020,"\t")
if (pvalue_N_G2020 < 0.05) cat("-> significative") else
  cat("-> not significative")

```

```{r}
hist(MD_perm_G2020, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(whole network permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0.1,0.4))
abline(v=mean(obs_value_G2020), col="red", lwd=3)
```
# GB2 2021 ---------------------

```{r}
GB2_2021<-read.csv("CSV_Dayroosts_GB2_2021_juv.csv",header=TRUE, row.names=NULL,sep=";",check.names = F)

GB2_2021<-GB2_2021[c(1:56)] #filter columns
GB2_2021<-GB2_2021[1:37,]

GB2_2021 <- GB2_2021 %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ replace(., . == "", NA)))

colnames(GB2_2021)[colnames(GB2_2021) == "Date"] <- "date"
```

```{r}
colnames(GB2_2021)<-as.character(colnames(GB2_2021))
data_G2021<- GB2_2021%>%
  mutate(across(-date, as.character)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(date),  
    names_to = "ID",                     
    values_to = "box"                       
  ) %>%
  drop_na(box)

#group = date+box
data_G2021$daygroup<-as.factor(paste(data_G2021$date, data_G2021$box, sep="_"))

ind_G2021<-data.table(ID=c(data_G2021$ID), group=data_G2021$daygroup, day=c(data_G2021$date)) #df with 3 col: ID, group and day 
GBI_G2021<- get_group_by_individual(ind_G2021, data_format = "individuals") #get group by individual matrix with 0 or 1 when present
GBI_G2021<- as.matrix(GBI_G2021)
```

## network
```{r}
net_G2021<-get_network(GBI_G2021,data_format = "GBI",association_index = "SRI")
```
```{r}
g_G2021<- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(net_G2021,mode = "undirected",weighted = TRUE,diag = FALSE) 
plot(g_G2021,vertex.label = V(g_G2021)$name,edge.width = E(g_G2021)$weight * 6, layout = layout_with_fr, vertex.color = "lightblue",edge.color = "plum3") 
```
#####plot + threshold
```{r}
threshold_G2021<- mean(net_G2021, na.rm = TRUE) +  sd(net_G2021, na.rm = TRUE)

gfilter_G2021<- delete_edges(g_G2021, E(g_G2021)[weight <threshold_G2021]) #either the threshold or the mean? depending on the graph wanted
plot(gfilter_G2021, edge.width = E(gfilter_G2021)$weight*5,edge.curved=T,vertex.color = "lightblue", vertex.size = 15, vertex.label.cex = 0.7,edge.color = "plum3")
```

##vertex attributes
```{r}
mother_daughter_pairs_G2021<-data.frame(mother=c("AC35","41C1","076C","F44A","44BD","D629","C5F2","AA3C","15BF"),daughter=c("A816","34C9","494F","9A80","0734","8106","0145","A42D","8BE4"))

mother_daughter_pairs_G2021$pairID<-c(1:nrow(mother_daughter_pairs_G2021))

mother_ids_G2021<- mother_daughter_pairs_G2021$mother
daughter_ids_G2021<- mother_daughter_pairs_G2021$daughter

V(g_G2021)$type <- "other"  # default value
V(g_G2021)[V(g_G2021)$name %in% mother_ids_G2021]$type   <- "mother-daughter"
V(g_G2021)[V(g_G2021)$name %in% daughter_ids_G2021]$type <- "mother-daughter"

plot(gfilter_G2021, edge.width = E(gfilter_G2021)$weight*6,layout = layout_with_fr,vertex.color = ifelse(V(g_G2021)$type == "mother-daughter",   "darkcyan", "gray"),vertex.label = V(g_G2021)$name,edge.color="plum3")
```
```{r}
md_lookup_G2021<- mother_daughter_pairs_G2021%>%pivot_longer(cols = c("mother","daughter"), values_to = "ID") %>%select(ID, pairID)
#pivot the matrix so that each ID has the pair ID separately

IDs_G2021<- match(V(g_G2021)$name, md_lookup_G2021$ID)   # returns NA if the name is not found
V(g_G2021)$pairID <- md_lookup_G2021$pairID[IDs_G2021] 

pair_IDs_G2021<- factor(V(g_G2021)$pairID)

# Create a palette for however many pairIDs you have (excluding NA)
n_pairs_G2021<-length(levels(na.omit(pair_IDs_G2021)))
my_palette_G2021<-brewer.pal(n = max(3,n_pairs_G2021), name = "Paired") 

# Map each vertex to the correct color
pair_colors_G2021<- my_palette_G2021[pair_IDs_G2021]
pair_colors_G2021[is.na(pair_colors_G2021)] <- "gray80"
```

##big plot
```{r}
plot(gfilter_G2021, edge.width = E(gfilter_G2021)$weight*6,edge.curved=F,vertex.color =pair_colors_G2021, vertex.size = 15, vertex.label.cex = 0.7)
pair_levels_G2021<- levels(na.omit(pair_IDs_G2021))
legend_colors_G2021 <- my_palette_G2021[seq_along(pair_levels_G2021)]

legend(
  "topleft",
  legend = c(paste("Pair", pair_levels_G2021), "random pair"),
  col    = c(legend_colors_G2021, "gray80"),
  pch    = 19,
  pt.cex = 2,
  bty    = "n"
)
```
###edge list
```{r}
edge_G2021<-  igraph::as_data_frame(g_G2021, what="edges")

vtype_G2021<- V(g_G2021)$type
names(vtype_G2021) <- V(g_G2021)$name

from_type_G2021<-vtype_G2021[ edge_G2021$from ]
to_type_G2021<-vtype_G2021[ edge_G2021$to   ]

edge_G2021$pair_type <- ifelse(
  from_type_G2021== "mother-daughter" & to_type_G2021 == "mother-daughter",
  "mother-daughter",
  "other"
)
```

### SRI plot
```{r}
sri_G2021<- as.vector(net_G2021)
sri_G2021 <- sri_G2021[sri_G2021 > 0]  # Remove zero values
meansri_G2021<- mean(sri_G2021)


hist(sri_G2021, breaks=30, main="Distribution of association index (SRI) for GB2 in 2021", 
     xlab="Association index (SRI)", ylab="Frequency", col="lightblue", border="black",xlim = (0:1))

#red line for the mean
abline(v=meansri_G2021, col="blue", lwd=2)
```
Two different hist for the two types of pairs
```{r}

```
## EDGE PERMUTATION
Metrics of interest for quantifying: 
1- The mean edge weight among mother–daughter vertices
2- the difference in average association between mother–daughter pairs versus all other pairs

1-
observed value
```{r}
mean_pair_association <- function(g) {
  pairid <- V(g)$pairID #new variable with the pair ID
  names(pairid) <- V(g)$name #get the pair ID individual's name 
  #find the pairID of both individual
  from_pairid<- pairid[ edge_G2021$from ]
  to_pairid<- pairid[ edge_G2021$to   ]
  
  #has to have the same ID and not NA
  MDpairs <- (from_pairid == to_pairid) & !is.na(from_pairid)
  
  #isolate the weight of association and measure mean
  md_weights <- edge_G2021$weight[MDpairs]
  mean(md_weights, na.rm=TRUE)
}
obs_value_G2021<- mean_pair_association(g_G2021)
```

permutation
```{r}
num_permutations<- 5000
perm_E_G2021<-numeric(num_permutations)

for (i in seq_len(num_permutations)) {
  # Randomly permute the pair_type labels
  permuted_edge_list<-edge_G2021
  permuted_edge_list$pair_type <- sample(edge_G2021$pair_type)
  
  # Calculate the statistic for permuted mother-daughter pairs
  permuted_md_edges <- permuted_edge_list[permuted_edge_list$pair_type == "mother-daughter", ]
  perm_E_G2021[i] <- mean(permuted_md_edges$weight, na.rm = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
pvalue_e_G2021<-mean(perm_E_G2021>= obs_value_G2021)

cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2021,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(perm_E_G2021),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_e_G2021, "\t")
if (pvalue_e_G2021< 0.05) cat("-> significative") else
  cat("-> not significative")

```
```{r}
hist(perm_E_G2021, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(edge label permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0.2,0.5))
abline(v=obs_value_G2021, col="red", lwd=3)
```
Good, however: maybe a vertex-label permutation would be better than a edge-label because mother-daughter is a fundamental property of individuals.

In edge-label, we shuffle which edges are labeled "MD", and in a vertex, we would shuffle the type of the vertex and then redefine the MD edges

vertex-label permutations best capture the idea that we’re testing whether these specific mother–daughter individuals have unusually strong connections compared to any random subset of individuals
But we would measure all the mother with all the mothers association?

## VERTEX PERMUTATION

I already calculated the observed value

permutation
```{r}
original_pairid_G2021<- V(g_G2021)$pairID

num_perm<- 5000
perm_V_G2021<- numeric(num_perm)

for (i in seq_len(num_perm)) {
  #shuffle the entire vector of pairIDs
  new_pid<- sample(original_pairid_G2021, replace = FALSE)
  
  #copy the original graph and assign the new id
  g_perm <- g_G2021
  V(g_perm)$pairID <- new_pid
  
  #mean association
  perm_V_G2021[i] <- mean_pair_association(g_perm)
}

```

```{r}
pvalue_v_G2021<- mean(perm_V_G2021>= obs_value_G2021)
cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2021,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(perm_V_G2021),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_v_G2021, "\t")
if (pvalue_v_G2021< 0.05) cat("-> significative") else
  cat("-> not significative")
```

```{r}
hist(perm_V_G2021, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(vertex label permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0,0.6))
abline(v=obs_value_G2021, col="red", lwd=3)
```

##DATA RANDOMIZATION 
(randomization of the entire network, then calculation of the differences between md pairs and non-md pairs to see if it is different than what is observed)
mean of association for mother-daughter pairs is already measured

permute
```{r}
network_perm_G2021<-network_permutation(association_data=GBI_G2021,data_format = "GBI",permutations=5000,association_index = "SRI",association_matrix = net_G2021,identities=colnames(GBI_G2021),days= 1:nrow(GBI_G2021))

```

```{r}
individuals_G2021<-colnames(GBI_G2021)
dimnames(network_perm_G2021)[[2]] <- individuals_G2021
dimnames(network_perm_G2021)[[3]] <- individuals_G2021
#network_perm[1,,]

md_mean_from_adj <- function(adj_mat, md_pairs) {
  # 'adj_mat': a 26x26 adjacency matrix with row/col names = individual IDs
  # 'md_pairs': a data frame with columns "mother", "daughter" 
  #    e.g. mother = "B587", daughter = "5672", etc.

  n_pairs <- nrow(md_pairs)
  vals <- numeric(n_pairs)

  for (i in seq_len(n_pairs)) {
    mother_id <- md_pairs$mother[i]
    daughter_id <- md_pairs$daughter[i]

    # match them to the adjacency
    row_idx <- match(mother_id, rownames(adj_mat))
    col_idx <- match(daughter_id, colnames(adj_mat))

    # If they exist in the names, get the weight
    # If either is NA, the pair is not found, so set to NA
    if (!is.na(row_idx) && !is.na(col_idx)) {
      vals[i] <- adj_mat[row_idx, col_idx]
    } else {
      vals[i] <- NA
    }
  }

  # Return the mean edge weight across all real MD pairs
  mean(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
}

```

```{r}
#measure the association only for the md in the entire permutations
MD_perm_G2021<-apply(network_perm_G2021, 1, function(adj_mat) {
  md_mean_from_adj(adj_mat, mother_daughter_pairs_G2021)
})

pvalue_N_G2021<- mean(MD_perm_G2021>= obs_value_G2021)

cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2021,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(MD_perm_G2021),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_N_G2021,"\t")
if (pvalue_N_G2021 < 0.05) cat("-> significative") else
  cat("-> not significative")

```

```{r}
hist(MD_perm_G2021, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(whole network permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0.2,0.55))
abline(v=mean(obs_value_G2021), col="red", lwd=3)
```
# GB2 2022 ---------------------

```{r}
GB2_2022<-read.csv("CSV_Dayroosts_GB2_2022_juv.csv",header=TRUE, row.names=NULL,sep=";",check.names = F)

GB2_2022<-GB2_2022[c(1:60)]#filter columns

GB2_2022 <-GB2_2022 %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ replace(., . == "", NA)))

GB2_2022<-GB2_2022[, !(colnames(GB2_2022) %in% "15BF")]

colnames(GB2_2022)[colnames(GB2_2022) == "Date"] <- "date"
```

```{r}
colnames(GB2_2022)<-as.character(colnames(GB2_2022))
data_G2022<- GB2_2022%>%
  mutate(across(-date, as.character)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(date),  
    names_to = "ID",                     
    values_to = "box"                       
  ) %>%
  drop_na(box)

#group = date+box
data_G2022$daygroup<-as.factor(paste(data_G2022$date, data_G2022$box, sep="_"))

ind_G2022<-data.table(ID=c(data_G2022$ID), group=data_G2022$daygroup, day=c(data_G2022$date)) #df with 3 col: ID, group and day 
GBI_G2022<- get_group_by_individual(ind_G2022, data_format = "individuals") #get group by individual matrix with 0 or 1 when present
GBI_G2022<- as.matrix(GBI_G2022)
```

## network
```{r}
net_G2022<-get_network(GBI_G2022,data_format = "GBI",association_index = "SRI")
```
```{r}
g_G2022<- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(net_G2022,mode = "undirected",weighted = TRUE,diag = FALSE) 
plot(g_G2022,vertex.label = V(g_G2022)$name,edge.width = E(g_G2022)$weight * 6, layout = layout_with_fr, vertex.color = "lightblue",edge.color = "plum3") 
```
#####plot + threshold
```{r}
threshold_G2022<- mean(net_G2022, na.rm = TRUE) +  sd(net_G2022, na.rm = TRUE)

gfilter_G2022<- delete_edges(g_G2022, E(g_G2022)[weight <threshold_G2022]) #either the threshold or the mean? depending on the graph wanted
plot(gfilter_G2022, edge.width = E(gfilter_G2022)$weight*5,edge.curved=T,vertex.color = "lightblue", vertex.size = 15, vertex.label.cex = 0.7,edge.color = "plum3")
```

##vertex attributes
```{r}
mother_daughter_pairs_G2022<-data.frame(mother=c("41C1","BF04","879F","E063","22B0","663E","F44A","A539","44BD","D629","AA3C","5939","2D31","D4DC","418B","8787","A816","F377","D14D"),daughter=c("DFAD","0636","12D7","0F3D","CC06","8C52","0896","922C","FB4B","F4D9","054D","0A62","8B80","F948","B484","D6D5","B0C8","EB80","BA7C"))

mother_daughter_pairs_G2022$pairID<-c(1:nrow(mother_daughter_pairs_G2022))

mother_ids_G2022<- mother_daughter_pairs_G2022$mother
daughter_ids_G2022<- mother_daughter_pairs_G2022$daughter

V(g_G2022)$type <- "other"  # default value
V(g_G2022)[V(g_G2022)$name %in% mother_ids_G2022]$type   <- "mother-daughter"
V(g_G2022)[V(g_G2022)$name %in% daughter_ids_G2022]$type <- "mother-daughter"

plot(gfilter_G2022, edge.width = E(gfilter_G2022)$weight*6,layout = layout_with_fr,vertex.color = ifelse(V(g_G2022)$type == "mother-daughter",   "darkcyan", "gray"),vertex.label = V(g_G2022)$name,edge.color="plum3")
```
```{r}
md_lookup_G2022<- mother_daughter_pairs_G2022%>%pivot_longer(cols = c("mother","daughter"), values_to = "ID") %>%select(ID, pairID)
#pivot the matrix so that each ID has the pair ID separately

IDs_G2022<- match(V(g_G2022)$name, md_lookup_G2022$ID)   # returns NA if the name is not found
V(g_G2022)$pairID <- md_lookup_G2022$pairID[IDs_G2022] 

pair_IDs_G2022<- factor(V(g_G2022)$pairID)

# Create a palette for however many pairIDs you have (excluding NA)
n_pairs_G2022<-length(levels(na.omit(pair_IDs_G2022)))
my_palette_G2022 <-  rainbow(19) 

# Map each vertex to the correct color
pair_colors_G2022<- my_palette_G2022[pair_IDs_G2022]
pair_colors_G2022[is.na(pair_colors_G2022)] <- "gray80"
```

##big plot
```{r}
plot(gfilter_G2022, edge.width = E(gfilter_G2022)$weight*6,edge.curved=F,vertex.color =pair_colors_G2022, vertex.size = 15, vertex.label.cex = 0.7)
pair_levels_G2022<- levels(na.omit(pair_IDs_G2022))
legend_colors_G2022 <- my_palette_G2022[seq_along(pair_levels_G2022)]

legend(
  "topleft",
  legend = c(paste("Pair", pair_levels_G2022), "random pair"),
  col    = c(legend_colors_G2022, "gray80"),
  pch    = 19,
  pt.cex = 2,
  cex = 0.6,
  bty    = "n"
)
```
###edge list
```{r}
edge_G2022<-  igraph::as_data_frame(g_G2022, what="edges")

vtype_G2022<- V(g_G2022)$type
names(vtype_G2022) <- V(g_G2022)$name

from_type_G2022<-vtype_G2022[ edge_G2022$from ]
to_type_G2022<-vtype_G2022[ edge_G2022$to   ]

edge_G2022$pair_type <- ifelse(
  from_type_G2022== "mother-daughter" & to_type_G2022 == "mother-daughter",
  "mother-daughter",
  "other"
)
```

### SRI plot
```{r}
sri_G2022<- as.vector(net_G2022)
sri_G2022 <- sri_G2022[sri_G2022 > 0]  # Remove zero values
meansri_G2022<- mean(sri_G2022)


hist(sri_G2022, breaks=30, main="Distribution of association index (SRI) for GB2 in 2022", 
     xlab="Association index (SRI)", ylab="Frequency", col="lightblue", border="black",xlim = (0:1))

#red line for the mean
abline(v=meansri_G2022, col="blue", lwd=2)
```
Two different hist for the two types of pairs
```{r}

```
## EDGE PERMUTATION
Metrics of interest for quantifying: 
1- The mean edge weight among mother–daughter vertices
2- the difference in average association between mother–daughter pairs versus all other pairs

1-
observed value
```{r}
mean_pair_association <- function(g) {
  pairid <- V(g)$pairID #new variable with the pair ID
  names(pairid) <- V(g)$name #get the pair ID individual's name 
  #find the pairID of both individual
  from_pairid<- pairid[ edge_G2022$from ]
  to_pairid<- pairid[ edge_G2022$to   ]
  
  #has to have the same ID and not NA
  MDpairs <- (from_pairid == to_pairid) & !is.na(from_pairid)
  
  #isolate the weight of association and measure mean
  md_weights <- edge_G2022$weight[MDpairs]
  mean(md_weights, na.rm=TRUE)
}
obs_value_G2022<- mean_pair_association(g_G2022)
```

permutation
```{r}
num_permutations<- 5000
perm_E_G2022<-numeric(num_permutations)

for (i in seq_len(num_permutations)) {
  # Randomly permute the pair_type labels
  permuted_edge_list<-edge_G2022
  permuted_edge_list$pair_type <- sample(edge_G2022$pair_type)
  
  # Calculate the statistic for permuted mother-daughter pairs
  permuted_md_edges <- permuted_edge_list[permuted_edge_list$pair_type == "mother-daughter", ]
  perm_E_G2022[i] <- mean(permuted_md_edges$weight, na.rm = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
pvalue_e_G2022<-mean(perm_E_G2022>= obs_value_G2022)

cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2022,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(perm_E_G2022),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_e_G2022, "\t")
if (pvalue_e_G2022< 0.05) cat("-> significative") else
  cat("-> not significative")

```
```{r}
hist(perm_E_G2022, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(edge label permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0.1,0.3))
abline(v=obs_value_G2022, col="red", lwd=3)
```
Good, however: maybe a vertex-label permutation would be better than a edge-label because mother-daughter is a fundamental property of individuals.

In edge-label, we shuffle which edges are labeled "MD", and in a vertex, we would shuffle the type of the vertex and then redefine the MD edges

vertex-label permutations best capture the idea that we’re testing whether these specific mother–daughter individuals have unusually strong connections compared to any random subset of individuals
But we would measure all the mother with all the mothers association?

## VERTEX PERMUTATION

I already calculated the observed value

permutation
```{r}
original_pairid_G2022<- V(g_G2022)$pairID

num_perm<- 5000
perm_V_G2022<- numeric(num_perm)

for (i in seq_len(num_perm)) {
  #shuffle the entire vector of pairIDs
  new_pid<- sample(original_pairid_G2022, replace = FALSE)
  
  #copy the original graph and assign the new id
  g_perm <- g_G2022
  V(g_perm)$pairID <- new_pid
  
  #mean association
  perm_V_G2022[i] <- mean_pair_association(g_perm)
}

```

```{r}
pvalue_v_G2022<- mean(perm_V_G2022>= obs_value_G2022)
cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2022,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(perm_V_G2022),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_v_G2022, "\t")
if (pvalue_v_G2022< 0.05) cat("-> significative") else
  cat("-> not significative")
```

```{r}
hist(perm_V_G2022, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(vertex label permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0,0.5))
abline(v=obs_value_G2022, col="red", lwd=3)
```

##DATA RANDOMIZATION 
(randomization of the entire network, then calculation of the differences between md pairs and non-md pairs to see if it is different than what is observed)
mean of association for mother-daughter pairs is already measured

permute
```{r}
network_perm_G2022<-network_permutation(association_data=GBI_G2022,data_format = "GBI",permutations=5000,association_index = "SRI",association_matrix = net_G2022,identities=colnames(GBI_G2022),days= 1:nrow(GBI_G2022))

```

```{r}
individuals_G2022<-colnames(GBI_G2022)
dimnames(network_perm_G2022)[[2]] <- individuals_G2022
dimnames(network_perm_G2022)[[3]] <- individuals_G2022
#network_perm[1,,]

md_mean_from_adj <- function(adj_mat, md_pairs) {
  # 'adj_mat': a 26x26 adjacency matrix with row/col names = individual IDs
  # 'md_pairs': a data frame with columns "mother", "daughter" 
  #    e.g. mother = "B587", daughter = "5672", etc.

  n_pairs <- nrow(md_pairs)
  vals <- numeric(n_pairs)

  for (i in seq_len(n_pairs)) {
    mother_id <- md_pairs$mother[i]
    daughter_id <- md_pairs$daughter[i]

    # match them to the adjacency
    row_idx <- match(mother_id, rownames(adj_mat))
    col_idx <- match(daughter_id, colnames(adj_mat))

    # If they exist in the names, get the weight
    # If either is NA, the pair is not found, so set to NA
    if (!is.na(row_idx) && !is.na(col_idx)) {
      vals[i] <- adj_mat[row_idx, col_idx]
    } else {
      vals[i] <- NA
    }
  }

  # Return the mean edge weight across all real MD pairs
  mean(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
}

```

```{r}
#measure the association only for the md in the entire permutations
MD_perm_G2022<-apply(network_perm_G2022, 1, function(adj_mat) {
  md_mean_from_adj(adj_mat, mother_daughter_pairs_G2022)
})

pvalue_N_G2022<- mean(MD_perm_G2022>= obs_value_G2022)

cat("Mean of observed association =",obs_value_G2022,"\n")
cat("Mean of permuted association =",mean(MD_perm_G2022),"\n")
cat("P-value is",pvalue_N_G2022,"\t")
if (pvalue_N_G2022 < 0.05) cat("-> significative") else
  cat("-> not significative")

```

```{r}
hist(MD_perm_G2022, breaks=30, col="lightblue",
     main="Distribution of the permuted mother-daughter association \n(whole network permutation)",
     xlab="Mean association among mother-daughter vertices (permuted)",xlim = c(0,0.2))
abline(v=mean(obs_value_G2022), col="red", lwd=3)
```
